# clawee — full documentation

> A fast, resumable remote-shell client: persistent PTY sessions on your machines, attached from anywhere over the burrowee transport.

clawee is the client; **claweed** is the session broker on the remote box. clawee speaks to your local burrowee daemon, which carries the connection through a relay and gateway to claweed. Sessions survive disconnects, reboots, and network roams — pick one back up exactly where you left it, from any device.

## Requirements

clawee is a client — it runs on top of **burrowee** (https://burrowee.com), the transport that carries your connection to the remote box. You need two things:

- **burrowee installed** — get it at https://burrowee.com. clawee talks to the local burrowee daemon over a unix socket and will auto-start it when needed (it never replaces or supervises it).
- **a paired gateway** — your device must be bootstrapped against at least one burrowee gateway. Without a gateway binding there is no route, and clawee will tell you so.

clawee deliberately owns no transport of its own. burrowee handles pairing, gateways, relays, and the encrypted tunnel; clawee focuses on the terminal — sessions, the picker, uploads, the composer.

Supported clients: macOS and Linux. A box that *hosts* sessions also runs `claweed` (the session broker).

## Install

Two components, each installed with a one-liner (fetched over TLS and signature-verified from release.clawee.org) and updated in place with its own `update` command.

Client — your laptop / workstation:

    curl -fsSL https://release.clawee.org/clawee/install.sh | sh
    clawee update            # update in place; skips if current
    clawee update --force    # reinstall the latest regardless

Daemon — boxes that host sessions:

    curl -fsSL https://release.clawee.org/claweed/install.sh | sh
    claweed update           # update in place; skips if current
    claweed update --force   # reinstall the latest regardless

## Pairing a device

Two things have to line up: a **route** — burrowee binds your device to a gateway — and **trust** — your clawee **device key** is enrolled on the box running `claweed`. clawee and claweed usually live on *different* machines, so the first device is enrolled by hand on the host; extra devices can then be approved remotely.

1. **Bind to a gateway — the route.** In your burrowee console, mint a one-time blob + PIN, then on the client:

       burrowee bootstrap <blob> <pin>

   This is transport only — it carries no trust on its own.

2. **Get your device key.** clawee mints `~/.clawee/id_ed25519` the first time it runs; the private key never leaves the client.

       clawee status                 # generates the key; shows the chain + fingerprint
       cat ~/.clawee/id_ed25519.pub  # the public key to enroll

3. **Send the public key to the host.** Copy that `id_ed25519.pub` line to the box running `claweed` — paste it, `scp` it, however you like. Only the *public* key travels.

4. **Enroll it on the host.** On the claweed box, register the key for your tenant. It enrolls under the host OS user you run it as — add `--user <name>` to target another (then connect with clawee's `-user <name>`):

       claweed auth @id_ed25519.pub        # from a file
       claweed auth "ssh-ed25519 AAAA… "   # or paste the key inline

5. **Connect.** Back on the client, open the picker and attach:

       clawee

**Additional devices — approve remotely (optional).** Once one device is enrolled you don't need host access for the next one: the new device *knocks* (it connects, gets refused as pending approval), then you approve it from the already-enrolled device — `clawee status` prints the exact line to run.

    clawee devices                              # enrolled keys + pending knocks
    clawee devices approve [--label <x>] <fp>   # enroll a pending device key
    clawee devices revoke <fp>                  # remove a key or drop a knock

## Attaching

Run `clawee` with no arguments to open the session picker and attach. Flags tune the bare invocation:

- `clawee` — open the local session picker and attach.
- `clawee <gateway>` — select a gateway by name, then pick a session (see Gateways).
- `-r` — resume the most-recently-attached session.
- `-reconnect-timeout <dur>` — per-attempt reconnect deadline (e.g. `5s`; default 5s).
- `-s <sid>` — attach a remote pty session id directly.
- `-user <name>` — gateway OS user to claim (default: `$USER`, or last-used).
- `-i <key>` — private key to authenticate with (default: ssh-agent, then `~/.clawee/id_ed25519`).
- `-socket <path>` — burrowee transport daemon socket (default: the burrowee default).
- `-transport <mode>` — force transport: `auto` | `ws` | `quic`.

## Commands

Every `clawee <command>` verb:

- `status` — probe the transport chain hop-by-hop: socket → daemon → relay → claweed → auth; also prints the clawee + claweed versions.
- `sessions [--watch]` — list the daemon's pty sessions; `--watch` streams live session events.
- `devices <list|approve|revoke>` — enroll and manage device keys (`approve [--label <x>] <fp>` / `revoke <fp>`).
- `relays <list|probe|priority|skip|use>` — rank relays (LAN-first); bare `relays` opens an interactive picker.
- `gw [use|list|name|user …]` — switch gateways (interactive; `l` = relay drill-down); `use <fp|name>`; `list`; `name`/`user <fp|name> <val>`.
- `doctor [--fix] [--yes]` — diagnose the local setup; `--fix` repairs what it can (`--yes` skips consent prompts).
- `update [--dry] [--force]` — self-update from release.clawee.org (shows the gap; skips if current).
- `lock-passphrase` — set the idle-lock passphrase in the local config.
- `version` — print the clawee version (also `--version`).
- `help` — print the full help (also `--help`, `-h`).

## Gateways

When your device is paired to more than one gateway, clawee scopes everything per gateway — sessions, relays, and the OS user. Pick one by short name, fall back to the chooser, or set friendly names.

- `clawee <name>` — attach scoped to that gateway (matches a short name, the gateway's name, or a fingerprint prefix).
- `clawee gw` — open the interactive gateway chooser (piped: list local sessions grouped by gateway, with live/dead state).
- `clawee gw use <fp|name>` — switch to that gateway and attach.
- `clawee gw list` — list all paired gateways with session counts.
- `clawee gw name <fp|name> <short>` — set a friendly short name for a gateway.
- `clawee gw user <fp|name> <user>` — set the per-gateway OS user (`-` clears the override).

With a single gateway, none of this shows — `clawee` goes straight to the picker. With more than one, bare `clawee` uses your last gateway or opens the chooser; press `g` in the picker to switch. In the chooser, `l` drills down into the selected gateway's relays and can pin one.

## In-session keys

Once attached, keystrokes pass through to the remote shell except:

- `Ctrl+]` — open the command composer (type a `/command` or text; Enter sends).
- `Ctrl+\` — lock the screen (unlock with the idle-lock passphrase).
- `F5` · `Ctrl+[` — redraw the remote screen (`Ctrl+[` needs the kitty keyboard protocol, e.g. Ghostty).
- `g` — in the session picker (not while attached): open the gateway chooser.

## Composer editing

Keys inside the command composer (opened with `Ctrl+]`):

- `Enter` · `Shift+Enter` — send the line · insert a newline (multi-line input; needs kitty-keyboard, e.g. Ghostty).
- Paste — multi-line pastes stay in the box; a pasted file path uploads the file; very large text is uploaded as a file too.
- `←` / `→` — move the cursor one character.
- `Home`/`End` · `Ctrl+A`/`E` — jump to the current line start / end.
- `Ctrl+Home`/`End` — jump to the buffer start / end (on a Mac laptop: `Fn+Ctrl+←`/`→`).
- `↑` / `↓` — move between lines; at the top/bottom edge, recall command history.
- `Backspace` / `Del` — delete before / after the cursor.
- `Ctrl+K` / `Ctrl+U` — kill to line end / to line start.
- `Ctrl+Backspace` — kill to line start (same as `Ctrl+U`).
- `Ctrl+L` · `Ctrl+C` — clear the current line · clear the box (stays open).
- `Tab` — complete the `/command`.
- `Esc` · `Ctrl+]` — close the composer.
- `Ctrl+\` — lock the screen.

## Slash commands

Inside the composer (`Ctrl+]`):

- `/upload` — send a file or directory — `/upload <path>` (also: paste a path or drop a file). Directories go as tar.gz; uploads land in a dated `uploads/<date>/…` dir on claweed ≥ v0.1.30.
- `/download` — fetch a remote file or dir to your machine — `/download <path>`.
- `/name` — set the session's display name — renames the remote pty.
- `/relays` — relay priority & skip — ↑↓ select · K/J reorder · s skip · u pin · a auto · p probe.
- `/settings` — show or set client upload limits — `/settings [max-upload|async-upload <size>]`.
- `/history` — page the remote session's command history (q to quit).
- `/version` — show the clawee + claweed build versions.
- `/restart` — restart the attached session in place.
- `/reconnect` — force a reconnect now.
- `/refresh` — repaint the remote screen now (alias `/redraw`).
- `/detach` — drop back to the session picker (the remote session keeps running).
- `/lock` — lock the screen now — `/lock [<passphrase>]`.
- `/passphrase` — set or clear the lock passphrase (masked entry).
- `/quit` — exit clawee (alias `/q`).

## Update

Both components self-update in place from the signed release channel:

    clawee update             # shows the version gap; skips if current
    clawee update --dry       # report only
    clawee update --force     # reinstall the latest regardless
    claweed update            # same, for the daemon

## Troubleshooting

- `clawee doctor [--fix] [--yes]` diagnoses the local setup and repairs what it can.
- `clawee status` probes the chain hop-by-hop (socket → daemon → relay → claweed → auth) and shows exactly where a connection stops.
- Docs: https://clawee.org/docs · Transport (pairing, gateways, relays): https://burrowee.com
